commit.message:
Unified PaperMCP: 30+ academic paper sources in one MCP server

Merged PaperMCP and openags/paper-search-mcp into a single unified project.

Sources (30+):
- ArXiv, PubMed, Semantic Scholar, CrossRef, OpenAlex, DBLP
- Google Scholar (scholarly + HTML), bioRxiv, medRxiv, Europe PMC, PMC
- HuggingFace, PapersWithCode, OpenReview, HAL, DOAJ, Zenodo
- OpenAIRE, INSPIRE-HEP, Unpaywall, IACR, CORE, CiteSeerX
- SSRN, BASE, ChemRxiv, Sci-Hub, IEEE (skeleton), ACM (skeleton)

Features:
- 4 unified MCP tools: search, download, read, list_papers
- CLI interface: paper-search command for terminal usage
- Docker support, Smithery deployment config
- Parallel search across all sources via unified dispatcher

perf: benchmark suite (15 categories, 33 benchmarks)

Compared against YamlDotNet and VYaml:
- POCO serialization/deserialization
- Block YAML serialization/deserialization
- DOM parsing
- Large document (200 entries)

- Kubernetes Deployment manifest

- Serialization: 15x faster
- Deserialization: 1.8-2.9x faster

YamlSerializer — public API:

- Smart quoting for special values

commit.message:
chore(*): use shared tsconfig base and TypeScript v6 (#981)

This updates the repo to consume the shared `tsconfig.base.json` pattern
used in `lumirlumir-configs` instead of duplicating compiler settings
across packages. It also moves TypeScript references to v6 and keeps
`eslint-config-bananass` on the latest published version required for
that upgrade.

- **Shared TypeScript base**
- Adds a root `tsconfig.base.json` aligned with the shared config
repository.
- Moves common compiler options out of package-level `tsconfig.json`
files and into the shared base.
- Keeps package-local overrides only where they are actually
package-specific (`rootDir`, `outDir`, test-only flags).

- **Reference sync updates**
- Updates `.github/sync-client.yml` to sync `tsconfig.base.json` to
`lumirlumir-configs`.
- Removes the old pattern of treating package `tsconfig.json` files as
the synced source of truth.

- **TypeScript v6 rollout**
  - Upgrades root and template TypeScript references from v5 to v6.
  - Refreshes the lockfile accordingly.
- Preserves `eslint-config-bananass` at the latest version during the
dependency update.

- **TypeScript example/template compatibility**
- Updates TypeScript example and template configs for the v6 behavior
change.
- Adds the minimal overrides needed for the example projects to continue
typechecking cleanly under v6.
- Adjusts the grade lookup example to avoid the stricter indexed-access
error surfaced by the new compiler/runtime combo.

Example of the config consolidation:

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "rootDir": "./src",
    "outDir": "build",
    "skipLibCheck": true
  }
}
```

---

References:

- https://github.com/lumirlumir/npm-eslint-markdown/pull/533
- https://github.com/lumirlumir/npm-clang-format-node/pull/603

commit.message:
fix: add Ctrl+J as universal newline key for multiline input

Most macOS terminals (Terminal.app, iTerm2) send identical \r for both
Enter and Shift+Enter — there's no way to distinguish them. Only Kitty
and WezTerm support the Kitty keyboard protocol (\x1b[13;2u).

Fix: Add Ctrl+J (\n / LF) as cross-terminal newline. In raw mode, Enter
sends \r (CR) while Ctrl+J sends \n (LF), making them distinguishable
on ALL terminal emulators.

Newline options:
- Ctrl+J: Works everywhere (recommended)
- Shift+Enter: Works on Kitty/WezTerm (Kitty protocol)
- Alt+Enter: Works on iTerm2 and some terminals
- Backslash + Enter: Always works (manual continuation)

Also: added multiline hint to banner UI.

fix: prevent 'Failed to fetch' on expired SWA auth sessions (#131)

* fix: prevent 'Failed to fetch' on expired SWA auth sessions (#130)

Root cause: When a user's SWA authentication cookie expires mid-session,
API calls to /api/converse get a 302 redirect to Azure AD's login page.
The browser's fetch() follows this cross-origin redirect, but Azure AD
doesn't return CORS headers, so the browser throws TypeError: Failed to
fetch — an opaque error with no actionable context for the user.

Fix:
- Add apiFetch() wrapper that uses redirect:'manual' to prevent the
  browser from silently following auth redirects across origins
- Detect opaque redirect responses and throw SessionExpiredError with
  a clear message instead of the cryptic 'Failed to fetch'
- Auto-redirect users to the SWA login page on session expiry
- Add error field to StreamEvent so SSE error events from the server
  are properly surfaced instead of silently swallowed
- Apply apiFetch to all authenticated API endpoints (converse,
  inspirations, playground)

Closes #130

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

resolveChainedPointer: any string starting with '/' is followed,

commit.message:
Add dependency CVE scanner via OSV.dev

New 'deps' command that scans NuGet and npm package manifests for
known vulnerabilities using the OSV.dev API.

Features:
- Manifest discovery: *.csproj, *.fsproj, *.vbproj, packages.lock.json,
  package-lock.json (v1/v2/v3), package.json
- Central package management: Directory.Packages.props with VersionOverride
- 2-step OSV lookup: querybatch for IDs, then /v1/vulns/{id} for details
- Graceful degradation: warns on network errors, doesn't fail the scan
- Reuses existing Finding type, output formatters, and CI integration
- New --ecosystem flag to filter by ecosystem (e.g., nuget,npm)

Modules:
- src/Deps/ManifestParser.fs — discovers and parses package manifests
- src/Deps/OsvClient.fs — OSV.dev HTTP client with batch queries
- src/Deps/DepScanner.fs — orchestrates parsing → querying → normalization

Tested against ZTS: 402 deps across 34 manifests, 7 real CVEs found
with severity levels and fix version suggestions.

commit.message:
fix: handle async FSWatcher errors in project customization watcher

On Linux, fs.watch() on a non-existent directory emits an asynchronous
'error' event instead of throwing synchronously. Without an error handler,
this crashes the process. Tests that create AryxAppService with fake
project paths (e.g. C:\workspace\project-one) trigger this on Linux CI
because those paths don't exist.

Attach an 'error' event handler to the FSWatcher that logs a warning and
closes the watcher gracefully. This fixes the Linux CI 'bun test' failure
and also prevents production crashes if a watched directory is deleted
at runtime.
